violations.description: Design and construction of floors, walls, and ceilings. — --Floors, walls, and/or ceiling * were not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent, and easily cleanable. - Flooring tile is broken and not sealed making it unable to properly clean.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…)* were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. - Floors are dirty and require a thorough cleaning.

General employee cleanliness. — --Food employee did not wash hands as necessary. Food employee was observed putting on gloves without washing his hands first. Hands should be washed before putting on gloves.

Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --Cloth for wiping food spills was not stored in sanitizing solution (at proper concentration) between uses. Wiping cloth found on the counter near the bucket with sanitizing solution. Wiping cloths should be stored in the sanitizing solution between uses or laundered.

Time as a public health control. — --Food was not marked or identified with the time within which it must be cooked, served, or discarded. Food items in the self serve hot hold unit and the chicken in the hot case were not marked with the time of preparation or disposal. Employee stated that the items are held for about two hours. Items should be marked with time whenever time is used as a control. ***Chicken and cheeseburger found at 133-136F, prepared after lunch. Items not held at 140F or above can not be cooled and sold cold.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not maintained in good repair. Several floor tiles were found missing between the back door and the hand washing station. Flooring should be maintained in good repair.

Handling and storage of poisonous and toxic materials. — --Poisonous or toxic material was not stored in a manner to protect against contamination of food, equipment, utensils, linens or single-service items. Degreaser bottle found hanging on shelving unit above the scoop used in the ice bin. Cleaners and other toxic materials should be stored separate from items used for food service.
Handwashing, good hygienic practices (5 pts deducted)
Clean, proper use, storage, sanitizing solution (2 pts deducted)
Cooking and reheating to the proper time and temperature (5 pts deducted)
Design, construction, clean, maintained (1 pt deducted)
Clean, employee dressing area, cleaning equipment storage, living/sleeping quarters separate (1 pt deducted)
